evals.question_short: Materialize-mode SRF: where state must live

assertions.text: States the tuplestore + TupleDesc MUST live in rsinfo->econtext->ecxt_per_query_memory, NOT CurrentMemoryContext
Explains *why*: CurrentMemoryContext at fmgr entry is typically a per-tuple ExprContext that is reset between tuple cycles
Recommends the InitMaterializedSRF(fcinfo, flags) helper as the canonical/preferred pattern
Mentions that Materialize-mode ignores the Datum return value, so PG_RETURN_NULL() is the convention
Mentions MAT_SRF_BLESS / MAT_SRF_USE_EXPECTED_DESC flags for RECORD or caller-expected descriptors
Cites a specific file:line or named knowledge file for the rule (funcapi.c:100-122 or knowledge/idioms/fmgr.md §8)
Calls out that this is the single most common SRF pitfall / flags it as a known footgun

evals.question_short: SPI_prepare on fn_extra segfaults second call

assertions.text: Diagnoses that a bare SPI_prepare's plan lives in the SPI Proc context and is freed by SPI_finish
Prescribes SPI_keepplan(plan) before SPI_finish, noting it reparents under CacheMemoryContext
Notes SPI_keepplan is one-way (saved=true) and pins underlying CachedPlanSources
Warns that the cache struct hung on fn_extra must be allocated in flinfo->fn_mcxt, not CurrentMemoryContext
Mentions that SRF value-per-call mode uses fn_extra itself (SRF_IS_FIRSTCALL tests fn_extra==NULL) so it cannot be shared
Mentions SPI_prepare returns NULL on failure and the code lands in the global SPI_result
Cites a specific file:line or named knowledge file for the keepplan/Proc-context rule
Identifies AtEOSubXact_SPI as the cleanup hook that frees tuptables/executor state from the dying subxact
States explicitly that SPI_tuptable created inside the failed subxact has been freed and touching it after rollback is use-after-free
Prescribes CopyErrorData BEFORE FlushErrorState to capture diagnostics, allocated in the saved oldctx
Mentions that SPI stack frames opened INSIDE the dying subxact are also popped (cannot continue SPI in them)
Shows the full canonical subxact pattern (oldctx + oldowner save, BeginInternalSubTransaction, PG_TRY/PG_CATCH, restore CurrentResourceOwner)
Connects this to why SPI_commit/SPI_rollback are forbidden inside a PL exception block (same root: open subxact)
Cites a specific file:line (spi.c:482-572) or knowledge/idioms/spi.md §9 for the cleanup rule
